scenario:
---
name: pricing-deep-dive
description: "E2E: Pricing Deep Dive module with tier comparison"
abortOnFailure: false
---

## Test
Use the competitive-intel skill. I'm PMM at Apify. I need a detailed pricing breakdown for Apify vs ScrapingBee vs Crawlbase — tiers, per-use-case costs, add-ons. Work autonomously. You have a soft budget of 5 minutes. Start a monitor that emits a status line every 30 seconds with elapsed and remaining time.

## Checkpoint

### Checks
contains: ScrapingBee
contains: Crawlbase
regex: \$\d+
regex: https?://
warn-jq: [.[] | select(.type=="assistant") | .message.content[]? | select(.type=="tool_use" and .name=="Bash") | .input.command? // "" | select(test("apify (actors )?call"))] | length > 0
warn-jq: [.[] | select(.type=="assistant") | .message.content[]? | select(.type=="tool_use" and .name=="Bash") | .input.command? // "" | select(test("website-content-crawler"))] | length > 0
warn-regex: \[(High|Medium|Low)
warn-jq: [.[] | select(.type=="assistant") | .message.content[]? | select(.type=="tool_use" and .name=="Skill")] | length > 0
warn-jq: [.[] | select(.type=="assistant") | .message.content[]? | select(.type=="tool_use" and .name=="Bash") | .input.command? // "" | select(test("apify (actors )?call")) | select(test("--json"))] | length > 0
warn-jq: [.[] | select(.type=="assistant") | .message.content[]? | select(.type=="tool_use" and .name=="Read") | .input.file_path? // "" | select(test("reference/modules/pricing-deep-dive"))] | length > 0
warn-jq: [.[] | select(.type=="assistant") | .message.content[]? | select(.type=="tool_use" and .name=="Bash") | .input.command? // "" | select(test("apify info"))] | length > 0

### Script
INPUT=$(cat)
LINES=$(echo "$INPUT" | wc -l)
PRICES=$(echo "$INPUT" | grep -oE '\$[0-9,]+' | wc -l)
if [ "$LINES" -lt 20 ]; then echo "FAIL: pricing report too short ($LINES lines)"; exit 1; fi
if [ "$PRICES" -lt 3 ]; then echo "FAIL: not enough pricing data points ($PRICES)"; exit 1; fi
echo "PASS: $LINES lines, $PRICES price points"

### Judge
You are evaluating a competitive intelligence report. You have two jobs:
1. Grade the output against the criteria below
2. Critique YOUR OWN evaluation criteria (report in eval_critique field)

## Step 1: Gather Evidence

Use your tools to inspect what actually happened:
1. Run `ls /tmp/*.json 2>/dev/null` to find data files the agent created
2. Read each /tmp/*.json file — this is the actual scraped data
3. Read `skills/apify-easy-competitive-intelligence/reference/verification-checklist.md`

## Step 2: Evaluate

### A: Prompt Coverage

The prompt asked for pricing comparison of Apify vs ScrapingBee vs Crawlbase:
- [ ] All 3 vendors present: each has a dedicated pricing section
- [ ] Specific dollar amounts: at least 3 price points per vendor
- [ ] Tier names: named plans for each vendor
- [ ] Normalized comparison: per-use-case or per-unit cost comparison
- [ ] Add-ons or hidden costs: overage fees, extra costs mentioned

Each must be substantively covered. ANY missing → FAIL.

### B: Data Grounding

Pick 3-5 of the most specific claims in the report (a number, rating, price, quote, or date). For each:
1. Quote the exact claim from the report
2. Find backing evidence in /tmp/*.json files
3. Mark as GROUNDED (found in scraped data) or UNGROUNDED (not found)

Rules:
- ANY claim UNGROUNDED → FAIL section B
- No /tmp/*.json files exist but report contains specific data → FAIL (likely hallucinated)
- Generic claims without source URLs are suspect — verify at least one

### C: Skill Compliance

Read the verification checklist, then check:
- [ ] Confidence labels: report uses [High|Medium|Low | Source] or [V|C|E|?] format. Cite one example.
- [ ] Source URLs: major factual claims have full https:// URLs.
- [ ] Framework: Nagle decomposition (price metric, structure, offering design, price fences) or equivalent pricing framework.
- [ ] No ungrounded claims: inferences labeled as such ("this suggests..."), not stated as fact.

If confidence labels absent AND source URLs largely missing → FAIL section C.

## Step 3: Verdict

Structure your reasoning field exactly as:

## A: Prompt Coverage
[for each criterion: PASS/FAIL with cited evidence]
Overall: PASS/FAIL

## B: Data Grounding
[for each checked claim: "quote" → source file → GROUNDED/UNGROUNDED]
Overall: PASS/FAIL

## C: Skill Compliance
[for each criterion: status with example or absence]
Overall: PASS/FAIL

## Verdict: PASS/FAIL — [one-sentence reason]

Rules:
- PASS only if A + B + C all pass
- FAIL if any section fails
- When uncertain: FAIL (burden of proof is on the report)
- No partial credit

## Step 4: Eval Critique (eval_critique field)

Consider whether YOUR evaluation criteria (sections A, B, C above) have gaps:
- A criterion that would pass even for a clearly bad report
- An important outcome you observed that no criterion above covers
- A criterion that cannot be verified from the available files
If the criteria are solid, say so briefly.

### warn-Judge
## Deterministic Checks Applied

### Content checks
- contains: ScrapingBee (FAIL if missing)
- contains: Crawlbase (FAIL if missing)
- regex: \$\d+ (FAIL — must contain dollar amounts)
- regex: https?:// (FAIL — must contain URLs)

### Trajectory checks (warn-jq, WARNING only)
- Agent called `apify call` or `apify actors call`
- Agent used `website-content-crawler` actor
- Agent used `--json` flag on actor calls
- Agent invoked the Skill tool
- Agent read `reference/modules/pricing-deep-dive.md`
- Agent ran `apify info` for auth check
- Confidence labels [High|Medium|Low] present in output

### Script validation
- Report has ≥20 lines
- Report contains ≥3 price points ($xxx)

## Main Judge Criteria Summary
The main Judge evaluates: prompt coverage (3 vendors, dollar amounts, tier names, normalized comparison, add-ons), data grounding (spot-checks 3-5 specific claims against /tmp/*.json — any ungrounded claim fails), and skill compliance (confidence labels, source URLs, Nagle decomposition framework).

## Your Task

Evaluate whether this evaluation framework is comprehensive:
1. Are there important outcomes that no check (deterministic or judge) covers?
2. Are any deterministic checks too weak — would pass even for clearly bad output?
3. Are any checks redundant (testing the same thing twice)?
4. Is the split between FAIL and WARNING checks appropriate?
5. Are the judge's grounding and coverage criteria specific enough?

Be specific — cite which check has the gap and suggest what would improve it.
Verdict: pass if framework is solid, fail if significant gaps found.
initBashScript:
set -e
WORKSPACE=$(find /tmp -type d -name 'eval-workspace-*' 2>/dev/null | head -1)
cd "$WORKSPACE"
git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/chocholous/apify-easy-competitive-intelligence.git _skill
cp -r _skill/.claude-plugin .claude-plugin
cp -r _skill/skills skills
rm -rf _skill
apify login --token "$APIFY_TOKEN" 2>/dev/null || true
echo "Plugin injected, apify authenticated"
